Title: The New Years
Synopsis: It’s the early morning of January 1st, 2016. Óscar is feeling down: he’s gone to a nightclub with his ex-girlfriend, Vero, thinking he still has a chance with her. But he’s wrong. It’s the same nightclub where Ana works as a waitress. When the club closes, Ana and Óscar meet at a bar.

Title: Twin Peaks: A Limited Event Series
Synopsis: My log has a message for you.

Title: Mussolini: Son of the Century
Synopsis: Mussolini has just founded the Fasces of Combat when D’Annunzio takes Fiume. Mussolini hopes to exploit the feat of his poet on the field.

Title: Hal & Harper
Synopsis: Hal and Harper’s childhood house is on the market, and Dad’s got even bigger news that’s about to send all three on a journey through time.

Title: The Decalogue
Synopsis: The Ten Commandments, exact and uncompromising, literally cast in stone, continues to provide a source of moral conflict in contemporary society. In the ten part epic masterpiece, The Decalogue, Krzysztof Kieslowski examines the dilemma of fundamental sin in the lives of ordinary Warsaw citizens.

Title: Self-Portrait as a Coffee-Pot
Synopsis: South African artist William Kentridge investigates life in the studio. He imagines his studio as an enlarged head, where multiple dialogues occur between the artist and himself. Kentridge begins interviewing his double. Soon, the whole studio is populated by Kentridge’s many selves.

Title: Fanny and Alexander (Television Version)
Synopsis: Through the eyes of sister and brother Fanny and Alexander Ekdahl, the many ups-and-downs of the extended Ekdahl family at the turn-of-the-twentieth-century are put on display in the director’s 312-minute, full length version, made for Swedish television.

Title: Agnes Varda: From Here to There
Synopsis: Made as a miniseries for television, the project is based on the material filmed by Varda over two years as she travelled the globe—from Stockholm to St. Petersburg, Lisbon to Rio de Janeiro, Mexico City to Los Angeles—meeting with friends, artists, and fellow filmmakers.

Title: Scenes from a Marriage (Television Version)
Synopsis: Ten years of Marianne and Johan’s relationship are presented. We first meet them ten years into their marriage. He is a college professor, she a divorce lawyer. They say that they are happily married but there is a certain detached aloofness in the way they treat each other.

Title: True Detective: Season One
Synopsis: An anthology series in which police investigations unearth the personal and professional secrets of those involved, both within and outside the law.

Title: Berlin Alexanderplatz
Synopsis: Hulking, childlike Franz Biberkopf is released from prison and vows to become an “honest soul;” however, he soon finds himself embroiled in Berlin’s criminal underworld, amid the corrosive urban landscape of Weimar-era Germany. A fifteen-hour TV series, based on Alfred Döblin’s 1929 novel.

Title: Cosmos
Director: David Oyster, Richard Wells, Tom Weidlinger, David Kennard, Geoffrey Haines-Stiles, Gregory Andorfer
Synopsis: Astronomer Carl Sagan leads us on an engaging guided tour of the various elements and cosmological theories of the Universe.

Title: Blossoms Shanghai
Synopsis: Young and inexperienced Ah Bao begins his transformation into Mr. Bao, a prosperous entrepreneur and the star attraction of Huanghe Road. But success breeds enemies, and when a hit and run on New Year’s Eve puts him in the hospital, it’s up to his friend Ling Zi to discover who is behind the attack.

Title: O.J.: Made in America
Synopsis: The rise and fall of American football star, O.J. Simpson, from his days growing up in Los Angeles to his murder trial that polarized the country.

Title: Band of Brothers
Director: Phil Alden Robinson, Richard Loncraine, Mikael Salomon, David Nutter, Tom Hanks, David Leland, David Frankel, Tony To
Synopsis: Drawn from interviews with survivors of Easy Company, as well as their journals and letters, Band of Brothers chronicles the experiences of these men from paratrooper training in Georgia through the end of the war.

Title: Chernobyl
Synopsis: In April 1986 an explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant in the USSR becomes one of the world’s worst man-made catastrophes.

Title: World on a Wire
Synopsis: Somewhere in the future, a computer project called Simulacron is able to simulate a full featured reality. When project leader Henry Vollmer suddenly dies, his successor Dr. Fred Stiller experiences odd phenomena. There has to be someone behind all this. Could Simulacron be the one responsible?

Title: Persona
Synopsis: A man with diagnosis of Alzheimer’s, decides to punish the criminals.

Title: Normal People
Synopsis: Follows Marianne and Connell, from different backgrounds but the same small town in Ireland, as they weave in and out of each other’s romantic lives. (12 episodes in total)

Title: Over the Garden Wall
Director: Robert Alvarez, Larry Leichliter, Eddy Houchins, Ken Bruce, Nate Cash, Bert Youn
Synopsis: Two brothers find themselves lost in a mysterious land and try to find their way home.

Title: Planet Earth II
Director: Justin Anderson, Ed Charles, Fredi Devas, Chadden Hunter, Emma Napper, Elizabeth White
Synopsis: David Attenborough returns in this breathtaking documentary showcasing life on Planet Earth.

Title: The Century of the Self
Synopsis: Adam Curtis’ acclaimed BBC documentary series examines the rise of the all-consuming self against the backdrop of the Freud dynasty. Edward Bernays, the nephew of Sigmund Freud championed the idea that the mob, was a gathering of unconscious,

Title: The Best of Youth (Part 1)
Synopsis: A historical saga set in Italy spanning from 1966 to 2000, The Best of Youth focuses on the life and loves of two brothers as they cross an era of turbulent upheaval, from wild, revolutionary youth into adulthood.

Title: Planet Earth
Synopsis: Emmy Award winning, 11-episodes, 5-years in the making, the most expensive nature documentary series ever commissioned by the BBC, and the first to be filmed in high definition.

Title: The Beatles: Get Back
Synopsis: Documentary mini-series about the music group The Beatles featuring in-studio footage that was shot in early 1969 for the 1970 feature film ‘Let It Be.’

Title: Fargo: Season One
Director: Adam Bernstein, Matt Shakman, Randall Einhorn, Colin Bucksey, Scott Winant
Synopsis: In 2006, manipulative master criminal Lorne Malvo meets and forever changes the life of small-town insurance salesman Lester Nygaard, unleashing relentless destruction in a small Minnesota town.

Title: Heimat: A Chronicle of Germany
Synopsis: Heimat details the turbulent years between 1919 and 1982 through the eyes of the citizens of a small, fictional German village. The central character is Marita Breuer, who matures from a fresh-faced teen to a wrinkled, grim-visaged survivor of the best and the worst life has to offer.

Title: The Best of Youth (Part 2)
Synopsis: A historical saga set in Italy spanning from 1966 to 2000, The Best of Youth focuses on the life and loves of two brothers as they cross an era of turbulent upheaval, from wild, revolutionary youth into adulthood.

Title: Heimat II: A Chronicle of a Generation
Synopsis: The movie consist of 13 separate episodes each handling a period between 1960 and 1970. The length of these periods varies from one day to some years. It tells the story of a group of people in Munich (mostly music and film students).

Title: The Tatami Galaxy
Synopsis: Set in Kyoto, this is the secret confession of a mediocre college student about how which club you choose freshman year changes everything. It is an anthology of the paranormal revealing the parallel worlds of youths craving romance and happiness in the uncertain future.

Title: Serial Experiments Lain
Synopsis: Strange events begin to occur as a withdrawn girl named Lain becomes obsessed with interconnected virtual realm of “The Wired”.

Title: Mysteries of Lisbon
Synopsis: While attending a religious boarding school, Pedro da Silva, an orphan, discovers his mother’s identity: the Countess Angela de Lima. With the help of Father Dinis, Pedro’s real guardian and protector, the Countess is given a chance to get to know the child he was forced to abandon.

Title: Ping Pong
Synopsis: Five ping pong prodigies learn to navigate the harsh climate of competitive sport and the even more frightening realm of self realization.

Title: The Story of Film: An Odyssey
Synopsis: The Story of Film: An Odyssey, directed by film critic Mark Cousins, is the story of cinema told through the history of cinematic innovation. The film covers 6 continents and 12 decades, showing how filmmakers are influenced both by the historical events of their times, and by each other.

Title: Blue Planet II
Synopsis: Nearly a generation after the acclaimed Blue Planet documentary was released, David Attenborough returns to narrate this groundbreaking sequel/reboot. Blue Planet ll focuses more heavily on mankind’s influence on the world’s oceans through both global pollution and climate change.

Title: Our Planet
Synopsis: Documentary series, made for Netflix, focusing on the breadth of the diversity of habitats around the world, from the remote Arctic wilderness and mysterious deep oceans to the vast landscapes of Africa and diverse jungles of South America.

Title: Angels in America
Synopsis: Set in New York City during the mid-’80s, the story follows the interconnected lives of several people affected by the AIDS crisis, intense spiritual experiences, and the Reagan Administration.

Title: Ways of Seeing
Synopsis: A 1972 BBC four-part television series of 30-minute films created chiefly by writer John Berger and producer Mike Dibb.

Title: Olive Kitteridge
Synopsis: The story focuses on a middle-school math teacher Olive and her relationships with Henry which spans 25 years over the four parts.

Title: Eight Hours Don't Make a Day
Synopsis: The young toolmaker Jochen fights with his colleagues to a performance bonus. He gets to know the girl Marion. For both, it is love at first sight.

Title: The World at War
Director: Hugh Raggett, David Elstein, Ted Childs, Michael Darlow, John Pett
Synopsis: A series of accurate documentaries about World War II.

Title: Love Exposure (Television Version)
Synopsis: A bizarre love triangle forms between a young Catholic upskirt photographer, a misandric girl and a manipulative cultist.

Title: Doomed Love
Synopsis: A story about doomed love between two people from different worlds and the impact in their lives.

Title: The Vietnam War
Synopsis: Epic story of one of the most consequential, divisive, and controversial events in history as never told before on film. Visceral and immersive, exploring the human dimensions of the war through revelatory testimony of nearly 80 witnesses.

Title: When the Levees Broke
Synopsis: This intimate, heart-rending portrait of New Orleans in the wake of Hurricane Katrina tells the heartbreaking personal stories of those who endured this harrowing ordeal and survived to tell the tale of misery, despair and triumph.

Title: The Civil War
Synopsis: Ken Burns’ groundbreaking documentary on the American Civil War.

Title: Eight Deadly Shots
Synopsis: In remote Finland, good-natured farmer Pasi must provide for his wife and four children, supplementing his meager income with odd jobs. But when Pasi begins brewing moonshine deep in the woods with his friend Reiska, his wife becomes concerned that he is returning to his heavy drinking ways.

Title: The Young Pope
Synopsis: The beginning of the pontificate of Lenny Belardo, alias Pius XIII, the first American Pope in history.

Title: Samuel
Synopsis: Emotive depiction of adolescence follows Samuel and his friends, which mixes introspection, humor and nostalgic songs.

Title: Li'l Quinquin
Synopsis: It is the start of the summer holidays for Quinquin, and it is also the start of a local killing spree after a woman is chopped up and fed to a cow found dead in an old bunker.

Title: Once Upon a Time There Was a Country
Synopsis: Epic story of two friends to portray a Yugoslav history from the beginning of World War II until the beginning of Yugoslav Wars.

Title: When They See Us
Synopsis: Based on a true story that gripped the country, the limited series will chronicle the notorious case of five teenagers of color who were convicted of a rape they did not commit.

Title: FLCL
Director: Takeshi Ando, Masahiko Ôtsuka, Shôji Saeki, Kazuya Tsurumaki
Synopsis: A 12-year old boy named Naota one day meets a strange woman, riding a Vespa and wielding a big guitar. As soon as she appears, mysterious things start happening.

Title: Mononoke
Synopsis: A mysterious man called the Medicine Seller travels along feudal Japan, uncovering and slaying evil spirits called Mononoke.

Title: The Beatles Anthology
Director: Kevin Godley, Bob Smeaton, Geoff Wonfor
Synopsis: A documentary on the life and times of the Beatles. It features clips from many of their songs as well as in-depth descriptions of their songs, tours, and lives.

Title: Okupas
Synopsis: 4 young men with different backgrounds merge in the same house and have to face new experiences living in a stolen house, being prosecuted by the police and other gangs… Okupas (Squatters) is an Argentine TV show made in 2000. It’s one of the most acclaimed shows from that country.

Title: Sharp Objects
Synopsis: A reporter confronts the psychological demons from her past when she returns to her hometown to cover a violent murder.

Title: Masum
Synopsis: Cevdet, a retired commissioner, and his wife spend their pensions in peace on a farm away from the city. Suddenly the life of the family is destroyed by the sudden arrival of a terrible news from the little son.

Title: Cosmos: A Spacetime Odyssey
Synopsis: A documentary series that explores how we discovered the laws of nature and found our coordinates in space and time.

Title: Russia [1985-1999] TraumaZone
Synopsis: Block by block, Adam Curtis’ astonishing history uses archival footage to build a narrative of societal collapse, political opportunism, corruption and identity crisis in the USSR’s final hour.

Title: Manuel on the Island of Wonders
Synopsis: This three part French TV serial for children is the favourite of many devotees of Raúl Ruiz. It ties the enchantment and mystery of Lewis Carroll, Carlo Collodi and the Brothers Grimm to the filmmaker’s experiments with narrative strategies and what he calls the pentaludic model of storytelling.

Title: Brideshead Revisited
Synopsis: Two young men meet at Oxford. Charles Ryder, though of no family or money, becomes friends with wealthy Sebastian Flyte.

Title: Şaşıfelek Çıkmazı
Synopsis: Işleri hep ters gitsede yüzlerinden güleryüz eksik omayan aşk,dostluk,macera dolu olayların geçtiği sıcak bir mahalle dizisiydi şaşıfelek çıkmazı ayrıca güçlü kadın karakterleriyle akıllara yer etmiş bir dizidir.

Title: Can't Get You Out of My Head: An Emotional History of the Modern World
Synopsis: This new series of films by Adam Curtis tell the story of how Western societies have become split and polarised not just in politics but across the whole culture. And why both those in power – and we – find it so difficult to move on.

Title: The Singing Detective
Synopsis: The story of The Singing Detective unfolds in three time periods: the Forest of Dean in the 1930s, a 1980s hospital ward, and a film-noir fantasy London of the 1940s. The link between these ‘worlds’ is Philip Marlow, a writer of crime fiction recovering from psoriasis in hospital.

Title: The Blue Planet
Synopsis: Mammoth TV-mini-series, five years in the making, taking a look at the rich tapestry of life in the world’s oceans.

Title: The Hateful Eight: Extended Version
Synopsis: The “extended version” of Quentin Tarantino’s ‘The Hateful Eight’ consists of 4 episodes running about 50 minutes each.

Title: La mesías
Synopsis: The viral video of a Christian pop group made up of several sisters has an enormous effect on the life of Enric, a man tormented by a childhood marked by religious fanaticism and the yoke of a mother with messianic delirium.

Title: Maid
Director: John Wells, Helen Shaver, Nzingha Stewart, Lila Neugebauer, Quyen Tran
Synopsis: A single mother who turns to housekeeping to barely make ends meet as she battles against poverty, homelessness, and bureaucracy.

Title: Generation Kill
Synopsis: A Rolling Stone reporter, embedded with The 1st Recon Marines, chronicles his experiences during the first wave of the American-led assault on Baghdad in 2003.

Title: We Are Who We Are
Synopsis: Two American teenagers come of age while living on an American military base in Italy.

Title: The Jinx: The Life and Deaths of Robert Durst
Synopsis: Robert Durst, scion of one of New York’s billionaire real estate families, has been accused of three murders but never convicted. Brilliant, reclusive, and the subject of relentless media scrutiny, he’s never spoken publicly—until now.

Title: Fargo: Season Two
Director: Randall Einhorn, Michael Uppendahl, Noah Hawley, Jeffrey Reiner, Keith Gordon, Adam Arkin
Synopsis: In 1979, young State Trooper Lou Solverson investigates a case involving a local crime gang, a major mob syndicate, and a small-town beautician named Peggy Blumquist, along with her husband Ed, the local butcher’s assistant.

Title: The Last Dance
Synopsis: Charting the rise of the 1990’s Chicago Bulls, led by Michael Jordan, one of the most notable dynasties in sports history.

Title: 10 Years with Hayao Miyazaki
Synopsis: An exclusive 4-part documentary chronicling the creative process of the legendary Japanese filmmaker. He is shown as a passionate artisan, a steadfast trailblazer, and a father butting heads with his son.

Title: The Trial of Tim Heidecker
Synopsis: Full coverage of The Trial of Tim Heidecker in which Tim Heidecker stood accused of 20 counts of 2nd degree felony murder against The Electric Sun 20.

Title: Life Is a Miracle
Synopsis: The main character Luka is a somewhat eccentric engineer who builds a tunnel between the Balkans and the rest of the world. Luka’s wife is Jadranka – the singer who suffers from a sincere disease. Their son Milos dreams to become a football player, however he hasn’t enough talent for the game.

Title: I, Claudius
Synopsis: Follows the history of the Roman Empire, from approximately the death of Marcellus (24/23 BC) to Claudius’ own death in 54 AD. 13 episodes in total.

Title: Small Axe
Synopsis: The anthology consists of five films that tell distinct stories about the lives of West Indian immigrants in London from the 1960s to the 1980s.

Title: Top of the Lake
Synopsis: A 12-year-old girl stands chest deep in a frozen lake. She is five months pregnant, and won’t say who the father is. Then she disappears. So begins a haunting mystery that consumes a community.

Title: Pride and Prejudice
Synopsis: The timeless themes of love and marriage in Jane Austen’s superb romantic comedy have captivated readers for generations.

Title: Andor
Director: Toby Haynes, Susanna White, Benjamin Caron, Ariel Kleiman, Janus Metz Pedersen, Alonso Ruizpalacios
Synopsis: Prequel series to Star Wars’ ‘Rogue One’, following the adventures of Cassian Andor during the formative years of the Rebellion.

Title: Pretend It's a City
Synopsis: Wander the New York City streets and fascinating mind of wry writer, humorist and raconteur Fran Lebowitz as she sits down with Martin Scorsese.

Title: Kaiba
Synopsis: One day a man, Kaiba, wakes up in a broken room. He has no memories, only a pendant with a picture of an unknown woman.

Title: Skam: Isak
Synopsis: The story of young teenagers and pupils on Hartvig Nissens upper secondary school in Oslo, and their troubles, scandals and everyday life. Each season is told from a different person’s point of view. The main character of the third season is Isak.

Title: The House in the Woods
Synopsis: This television series happily combines a profound naturalism and a strange sense of fantasy, a liberty in its tone where hidden or manifested suffering alternates with an astonishing happiness to be alive.

Title: Adolescence
Synopsis: A family’s world turns upside down when 13-year-old Jamie Miller is arrested for murdering a schoolmate. The charges against their son force them to confront every parent’s worst nightmare.

Title: Life
Director: Martha Holmes, Simon Blakeney, Stephen Lyle
Synopsis: A nature documentary tv-mini-series made by BBC television, first broadcast as part of the BBC’s Darwin Season.

Title: Penance
Synopsis: Tragedy strikes when young Emili is shockingly murdered by a mysterious stranger. The other four girls present at the scene of the crime—Sae, Maki, Akiko, and Yuka—are unable to remember the killer’s face. The crime goes unsolved. Fifteen years later, Sae is paid a surprise visit on her wedding day.

Title: This Is England '88
Synopsis: Christmas 1988. Soulmates Woody and Lol find themselves in exile from each other and the gang. Trying to understand the definition ‘growing up’, Shaun begins a course at College, that quickly takes a wrong turn.

Title: The Night Of
Synopsis: After a night of partying with a female stranger, a man wakes up to find her stabbed to death and is charged with her murder.

Title: I Know This Much Is True
Synopsis: Middle-aged Dominick Birdsey recounts his troubled relationship with Thomas, his paranoid schizophrenic twin brother, and his efforts to get him released from an asylum.

Title: Riot Police
Synopsis: Six riot police proceed with an eviction in the centre of Madrid, but things go wrong and a man loses his life. An Internal Affairs team is given the task of investigating the facts and the six riot police are accused of manslaughter. The squad set about trying to find their own way out, leading to

Title: Rurouni Kenshin: Trust & Betrayal
Synopsis: In the era of Japan’s Meiji Restoration, an assassin regains his humanity.

Title: The Queen's Gambit
Synopsis: Set during the Cold War era, orphaned chess prodigy Beth Harmon struggles with addiction in a quest to become the greatest chess player in the world.

Title: Gintama: The Semi-Final
Synopsis: A quick look at everyone in Gintama before the finale.

Title: The Makanai: Cooking for the Maiko House
Director: Hirokazu Koreeda, Megumi Tsuno, Hiroshi Okuyama, Takuma Satô
Synopsis: Kiyo becomes a Makanai (person who cooks meals) at a house where Maiko (apprentice geishas) live together. The story depicts the everyday life of Kiyo maiko Sumire, her childhood friend who came with her from Aomori to Kyoto, amid a vibrant world of geisha and maiko courtesans.

Title: I Told Sunset About You
Synopsis: The story of two high school boys who are relearning the nature of their relationship through complicated and ever-changing feelings like a storm that blows the boat away from it’s path.

Title: Watchmen
Director: Nicole Kassell, Steph Green, Andrij Parekh, Stephen Williams, David Semel, Frederick E.O. Toye
Synopsis: A continuation of the acclaimed graphic novel, this HBO original series takes place in contemporary America, where costumed heroes are outlawed the doomsday clock continues to tick.

Title: Coincoin and the Extra-Humans
Synopsis: Li’l Quinquin is back! He is now a surly teenager called Coincoin and is drawn towards a rightwing nationalist group. Eccentric inspector Van Der Weyden is faced by an even more bizarre mystery that revolves around extra-terrestrial magma falling from the sky. The Extra-Human invasion has begun!

Title: Scenes from a Marriage
Synopsis: A series adaptation that re-examines Ingmar Bergman’s iconic depiction of love, hatred, desire, monogamy, marriage and divorce through the lens of a contemporary American couple.

Title: Wild Wild Country
Synopsis: When the world’s most controversial guru builds a utopian city deep in the Oregon desert, conflict with the locals escalates into a national scandal. A true story.

Title: This Is Going to Hurt
Synopsis: Set on labour ward with all its hilarity and heart-lifting highs but also its gut-wrenching lows, the show delivers a brutally honest depiction of life as a junior doctor on the wards, and the toll the job can take back home.

Title: Frozen Planet
Director: Chadden Hunter, Adam Scott, Jeff Wilson
Synopsis: Focuses on life and the environment in both the Arctic and Antarctic.

Title: Hollywood
Synopsis: Distilled from Kevin Brownlow’s 1968 book “The Parade’s Gone By…”, this 13-part mini-series follows the rise and fall of the American silent film industry Each episode deals with a specific aspect of Hollywood history, told through film clips and interviews with actors, directors and others.

Title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy
Synopsis: In the bleak days of the Cold War, espionage veteran George Smiley is forced from semi-retirement to uncover a Soviet agent within MI6’s echelons.

Title: Civilisation
Director: Michael Gill, Peter Montagnon, Ann Turner
Synopsis: Documentary series in which Sir Kenneth Clark examines the ideas and values which to him give meaning to the term Western Civilisation. — BFI Screen Online.

Title: Too Old to Die Young
Synopsis: A grieving police officer who, along with the man who shot his partner, finds himself in an underworld filled with working-class hit men, Yakuza soldiers, cartel assassins sent from Mexico, Russian mafia captains and gangs of teen killers.

Title: Mildred Pierce
Synopsis: Divorced single mom Mildred Pierce (Kate Winslet) decides to open a restaurant business, which tears at the already-strained relationship with her ambitious elder daughter, Veda, in Todd Haynes’ made-for-HBO mini-series adaptation of James M. Cain’s novel.

Title: Heated Rivalry
Synopsis: Two rival hockey stars at the top of their game face an unexpected challenge when they develop feelings for each other, complicating their careers and the sport’s expectations.

Title: Dr. Horrible's Sing-Along Blog
Synopsis: An aspiring supervillain must balance his career and his pursuit of a beautiful do-gooder.

Title: Veneno
Director: Javier Ambrossi, Javier Calvo, Alex Rodrigo, Mikel Rueda
Synopsis: Adored for her charisma and her free, explicit and fun way of expressing herself, La Veneno gained popularity thanks to her television appearances in the 90s. However, her life and especially her death remain an enigma.

Title: Millennium
Synopsis: This six-part Swedish TV series is based on the movies that draw from Stieg Larsson’s series of mystery novels about a reporter and a computer hacker. The series strikes a balance between being a thriller and a drama, with lots of suspense.

Title: David Lynch Presents Interview Project 1
Synopsis: Hit the road with David Lynch, his son Austin and the filmmaker Jason S., and meet the people they met. Selections from an ongoing series.

Title: La recta provincia
Synopsis: An ode to Chilean rural myth and storytelling. A poor mother and her simple son find a musical human bone. And then things take a turn for the oneiric.

Title: The Staircase
Synopsis: Granted unusual access to a suspected murderer’s lawyers, home and immediate family, de Lestrade’s cameras capture the defense team as it considers its strategic options. “The staircase” is an engrossing look at contemporary American justice that features more twists than a legal bestseller.

Title: The Terror: Season One
Director: Edward Berger, Tim Mielants, Sergio Mimica-Gezzan
Synopsis: The crew of a Royal Naval expedition searching for the Arctic’s treacherous Northwest Passage discovers instead a monstrous predator.

Title: She Loves to Cook, and She Loves to Eat
Synopsis: Cooking is how Nomoto de-stresses, but one day, she finds herself making way more than she can eat by herself. And so, she invites her neighbor Kasuga, who also lives alone. What will come out of this impromptu dinner invitation?

Title: John Adams
Synopsis: The life of one of the USA’s Founding Fathers, its second President, and his role in the nation’s first 50 years.

Title: Exterminate All the Brutes
Synopsis: The path to the new world, an unsettling and intricate story of genocides. Conquest, slavery and the fabrication of “Whiteness”.

Title: First Person
Synopsis: Errol Morris brings his unrivalled talents to the small screen for a stylized series of intimate interviews with a unique and fascinating array of people.

Title: Skam: Sana
Synopsis: The story of young teenagers and pupils on Hartvig Nissens upper secondary school in Oslo, and their troubles, scandals and everyday life. Each season is told from a different person’s point of view. The main character of the fourth and final season is Sana.

Title: Show Me a Hero
Synopsis: Nick Wasicsko takes office as mayor of Yonkers in 1987 and has to face the issue of building public housing in the white, middle-class side of town.

Title: Apocalypse: The Second World War
Synopsis: A six-part French documentary about the Second World War. The documentary is composed exclusively of actual footage of the war as filmed by war correspondents, soldiers, resistance fighters and private citizens.

Title: Shogun
Director: Jonathan van Tulleken, Frederick E.O. Toye, Takeshi Fukunaga, Hiromi Kamata, Charlotte Brandstrom, Emmanuel Osei-Kuffour, Jr., Justin Marks, Anthony Byrne, Kate Herron
Synopsis: Set in the 17th Century, John Blackthorne, a sailor who rises from outsider to samurai, while being used as a pawn in Japanese leader Toranaga’s struggle to reach the top of the ruling chain, or Shogun.

Title: The Death of Yugoslavia
Synopsis: An expansive, in-depth account of the dissolution of Yugoslavia and the ensuing Yugoslav wars, featuring extensive on-the-ground footage and interviews with the principal actors of the crisis.

Title: Wolf Hall
Synopsis: Henry VIII wants to annul his marriage of twenty years and marry Anne Boleyn. Into this impass steps Thomas Cromwell: a wholly original man, a charmer, and a bully, both idealist and opportunist, astute in reading people, and implacable in his ambition.

Title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
Director: Giles Badger, Chadden Hunter, Emma Napper, Fredi Devas
Synopsis: Millions of years ago incredible forces ripped apart the Earth’s crust creating our seven continents – each with its own distinct climate, its own distinct terrain and its own unique animal life, from the colorful paradise of South America to the scorching heat of Africa.

Title: Unbelievable
Director: Lisa Cholodenko, Susannah Grant, Michael Dinner
Synopsis: A teenager is charged with lying about having been raped, but two detectives follow the path to the truth.

Title: Baby Reindeer
Director: Weronika Tofilska, Josephine Bournebusch, Jon Brittain
Synopsis: The story follows the writer and performer Richard Gadd’s warped relationship with his female stalker and the impact it has on him as he is ultimately forced to face a deep, dark buried trauma.

Title: Fargo: Season Three
Director: Noah Hawley, Michael Uppendahl, John Cameron, Dearbhla Walsh, Mike Barker, Keith Gordon
Synopsis: In 2010, the twisted rivalry between brothers Emmit and Ray Stussy leads to murder and mayhem. Meanwhile, Emmit’s perfect life is further upended when he receives an unwelcome business proposal from a mysterious loner and true capitalist named V.M. Varga.

Title: Common Side Effects
Director: Camille Bozec, Sean Buckelew, Vincent Tsui
Synopsis: Former high school labmates Marshall and Frances unravel a conspiracy involving big pharma and the government to suppress knowledge of a rare fungus secret that may contain the solution to cure all the world’s diseases.

Title: Das Boot (Television Version)
Synopsis: A World War II German U-Boat crew have a terrifying patrol mission in the early days of the war.

Title: American Horror Story: Asylum
Director: Bradley Buecker, Michael Uppendahl, Alfonso Gomez-Rejon, David Semel, Michael Rymer, Michael Lehmann, Jeremy Podeswa, Craig Zisk
Synopsis: The second season of the anthology series created by Ryan Murphy. It centers on the fictional mental institution Briarcliff Manor, following the stories of the staff and inmates who occupy it.

Title: Patrick Melrose
Synopsis: Based on the Patrick Melrose series of semi-autobiographical novels written by Edward St. Aubyn. Melrose skewers the upper class as it tracks the protagonist’s harrowing odyssey from a deeply traumatic childhood through adult substance abuse and, ultimately, toward recovery.

Title: Feud: Bette and Joan
Director: Ryan Murphy, Gwyneth Horder-Payton, Helen Hunt, Liza Johnson, Tim Minear
Synopsis: Based on the legendary rivalry between Bette Davis and Joan Crawford which began early on their careers,climaxed on the set of “Whatever Happened to Baby Jane?” and evolved into an Oscar vendetta.

Title: This Is England '86
